<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 0.25in; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">Depreciation and amortization expense on totaled $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c0" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:UtilitiesOperatingExpenseDepreciationAndAmortization" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23767">2.0</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c9" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:UtilitiesOperatingExpenseDepreciationAndAmortization" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23768">0.9</ix:nonFraction>
million for the fiscal years ended December 29, 2024 and December 31, 2023. Finance leases are included within vehicles and makes up $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c249" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FinanceLeaseLiability" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23769">3.9</ix:nonFraction>
million of the total balance as of fiscal year ended December 29, 2024.</p>
<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">

</p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The Company recognized a total of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c0" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:GainLossOnDispositionOfAssets" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23770">3.8</ix:nonFraction> million
on impairment and loss on disposal of property and equipment for the fiscal year ended December 29, 2024 consisting primarily of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c250" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:GainLossOnDispositionOfAssets" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23771">3.4</ix:nonFraction>
million relating to its proprietary HelioTrackTM software system. The Company impaired the value of its HelioTrackTM software as this
software has no future use following the completion of the migration to software acquired in the SunPower Acquisition. There were no impairment
charges on tangible assets recognized for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.</p></ix:nonNumeric><div>

</div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c0" escape="true" name="us-gaap:PensionAndOtherPostretirementBenefitsDisclosureTextBlock" id="ixv-13317"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0"><b>(11) Employee Benefit Plan </b></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 0.25in; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The Company sponsors a 401(k) defined contribution
and profit-sharing plan (&#8220;401(k) Plan&#8221;) for its eligible employees. This 401(k) Plan provides for tax-deferred salary deductions
for all eligible employees. Employee contributions are voluntary. Employees may contribute the maximum amount allowed by law, as limited
by the annual maximum amount as determined by the Internal Revenue Service. The Company may match employee contributions in amounts to
be determined at the Company&#8217;s sole discretion. The Company made no contributions to the 401(k) Plan for the fiscal years ended
December 29, 2024 and December 31, 2023.</p></ix:nonNumeric><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

<table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;"> <tr style="vertical-align: top"> <td style="width: 24px; font-size: 10pt"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">(7)</span></td> <td style="text-align: justify; font-size: 10pt"><ix:footnote id="ix_23_footnote" xml:lang="en-US"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">Includes a gain of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c7" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DerivativeAssetsLiabilitiesAtFairValueNet" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23823">0.3</ix:nonFraction> million on the change in the fair value of derivative liabilities with related parties in the fiscal year ended December 29, 2024. Refer to Note 15 &#8211; Borrowings and Derivative Liabilities for details.</span></ix:footnote></td></tr> </table></ix:nonNumeric></ix:nonNumeric><div>
</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c0" continuedAt="_StockholdersEquityNoteDisclosureTextBlock-c0_cont_1" escape="true" name="us-gaap:StockholdersEquityNoteDisclosureTextBlock" id="ixv-13618"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0"><b>(13) Common Stock</b></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 0.25in; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The Company&#8217;s authorized capital stock comprises <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c5" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesAuthorized" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23824">1,000,000,000</ix:nonFraction>
shares of common stock and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c271" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:PreferredStockSharesAuthorized" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23825">10,000,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of preferred stock as of December 29, 2024. <span style="-sec-ix-hidden: hidden-fact-239">No</span> preferred stock has been issued and <span style="-sec-ix-hidden: hidden-fact-238">none</span> are
outstanding as of December&#160;29, 2024.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: center">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><i>Common Stock Purchase Agreements</i></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">On December 18, 2023, the Company entered into
separate common stock purchase agreements (the &#8220;Purchase Agreements&#8221;) with the Rodgers Massey Freedom and Free Markets Charitable
Trust and the Rodgers Massey Revocable Living Trust (each a &#8220;Purchaser&#8221;, and together, the &#8220;Purchasers&#8221;). Pursuant
to the terms of the Purchase Agreements, each Purchaser purchased <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c272" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodSharesAcquisitions" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23826">1,838,235</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock of the Company, par value $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c273" decimals="4" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:CommonStockParOrStatedValuePerShare" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23827">0.0001</ix:nonFraction>,
(the &#8220;Shares&#8221;), at a price per share of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c273" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:SaleOfStockPricePerShare" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23828">1.36</ix:nonFraction>, representing an aggregate purchase price of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c274" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="cslr:AggregatePurchasePrices" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23829">5.0</ix:nonFraction> million. The Purchasers paid
for the shares in cash. Thurman J. Rodgers is a trustee of each Purchaser, Executive Chairman of the Company&#8217;s board of directors
and Chief Executive Officer of the Company (&#8220;Rodgers&#8221; or &#8220;CEO&#8221;).</p></ix:nonNumeric><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"></p><div>

</div><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 86; Value: 1 --><div>
    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt">F-<!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->30<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
    </div><div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 12pt"><p style="margin: 0pt"></p></div><div>
    </div><!-- Field: /Page --><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><ix:continuation id="_StockholdersEquityNoteDisclosureTextBlock-c0_cont_1"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">On July 16, 2024, the Company entered into a common
stock purchase agreement with White Lion Capital, LLC (&#8220;White Lion&#8221;), as amended on July 24, 2024 (&#8220;White Lion SPA&#8221;),
and a related registration rights agreement for an equity line of credit financing facility. Pursuant to the White Lion SPA, the Company
has the right, but not the obligation, to require White Lion to purchase, from time to time up to $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c275" decimals="-6" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodValueNewIssues" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23830">30</ix:nonFraction> million in aggregate gross purchase
price of newly issued shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock, subject to the caps and certain limitations and conditions set forth
in the White Lion SPA, including terms that restrict the ability of the Company to issue shares of common stock to White Lion that would
result in White Lion beneficially owning more than <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c276" decimals="4" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="cslr:CommonStockOutstandingPercentage" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23831">9.99</ix:nonFraction>% of the Company&#8217;s outstanding common stock.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">On August 14, 2024, the Company entered into Amendment No. 2 to the
White Lion SPA (collectively with the White Lion SPA &#8220;White Lion Amended SPA&#8221;). The White Lion Amended SPA provides that the
Company may notify White Lion to exercise the Company&#8217;s right to sell shares of its common stock by delivering an Hour Rapid Purchase
Notice. If the Company delivers an Hour Rapid Purchase Notice, the Company shall deliver to White Lion shares of common stock not to exceed
the lesser of (i) five percent of the Average Daily Trading Volume on the date of an Hour Rapid Purchase Notice and (ii) <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c277" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesIssued" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23832">100,000</ix:nonFraction> shares
of common stock. The closing of the transactions under an Hour Rapid Purchase Notice will occur one Business Day following the date on
which the Hour Rapid Purchase Notice is delivered. At such closing, White Lion will pay the Company the Hour Rapid Purchase Investment
Amount equal to the number of shares of common stock subject to the applicable Hour Rapid Purchase Notice multiplied by the lowest traded
price of the Company&#8217;s common stock during the one-hour period following White Lion&#8217;s consent to the acceptance of the applicable
Hour Rapid Purchase Notice. Under the White Lion Amended SPA, the Company issued a total of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c278" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:CommonStockValue" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23833">2.9</ix:nonFraction> million shares of common stock for net
proceeds of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c23" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromIssuanceOfCommonStock" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23834">6.7</ix:nonFraction> million in the fiscal year ended December 29, 2024.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c0" escape="true" name="cslr:ScheduleOfReservedSharesOfCommonStockTableTextBlock" id="ixv-13650"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The Company has reserved shares of common stock
for issuance related to the following:</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 76pt">&#160;</p>

    warrants</span></td><td style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif">&#160;</span></td>

    <td style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: left"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif">&#160;</span></td><td style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: right"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif"><ix:nonFraction contextRef="c351" decimals="-3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DerivativeLiabilities" scale="3" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23849">1,310</ix:nonFraction></span></td><td style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: left"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif">&#160;</span></td></tr>
  <tr style="vertical-align: bottom; background-color: rgb(204,238,255)">
    <td style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: left"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif">Public

</div><ix:continuation continuedAt="cslr_WarrantsTextBlock-c0_cont_2" id="cslr_WarrantsTextBlock-c0_cont_1"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><i>Series D-7 Warrants (Converted to Common Stock
Warrants &#8220;Replacement Warrants&#8221;)</i></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In November 2022, the Company issued warrants
to purchase <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c279" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23858">656,630</ix:nonFraction> shares of Series D-7 preferred stock (the &#8220;Series D-7 warrants&#8221;) in conjunction with the Business Combination.
The warrant contained two tranches. The first tranche of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c280" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23859">518,752</ix:nonFraction> shares of Series D-7 preferred stock was exercisable at an exercise price
of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c280" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23860">2.50</ix:nonFraction> per share upon consummation of a merger transaction, or at an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c279" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23861">2.04</ix:nonFraction> per share upon remaining private and had
an expiration date of April 2024. The second tranche of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c281" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23862">137,878</ix:nonFraction> shares of Series D-7 preferred stock was exercisable at an exercise price
of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c281" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23863">5.00</ix:nonFraction> per share upon consummation of a merger transaction, or at an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c282" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23864">4.09</ix:nonFraction> per share upon remaining private and had
an expiration date of April 2024. The fair value of the Series D-7 warrants was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c283" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23865">2.4</ix:nonFraction> million as of July 18, 2023 when the warrants were
reclassified from redeemable convertible preferred stock warrant liability to additional paid-in capital, as the exercise price of the
warrants was fixed at $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c284" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23866">2.50</ix:nonFraction> per share of Complete Solaria Common Stock for the first tranche and $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c285" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23867">5.00</ix:nonFraction> per share of Complete Solaria Common
Stock for the second tranche upon the closing of the Mergers.</p>
<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">

</p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In October 2023, the Company entered into an Assignment and Acceptance
Agreement (&#8220;Assignment Agreement&#8221;), (refer to Note 15 &#8211; Borrowings and Derivative Liabilities). In connection with the
Assignment Agreement, the Company also entered into the First Amendment to Warrant to Purchase Stock Agreements with the holders of the
Series D-7 warrants. Pursuant to the terms of the agreement, the warrants to purchase <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c286" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23868">1,376,414</ix:nonFraction> shares of Series D-7 preferred stock converted
into warrants to purchase <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c287" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23869">656,630</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock (the &#8220;Replacement Warrants&#8221;). As a result of the warrant amendment,
the Company reclassified the Replacement Warrants from equity to liability. The Replacement Warrants were remeasured to fair value on
the amendment effective date and the Company recorded subsequent changes in fair value within Other income (expense), net in its consolidated
statements of operations and comprehensive loss.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The Replacement Warrants expired in April 2024
and the Company released the $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c288" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstanding" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23870">1.3</ix:nonFraction> million liability recognized in connection with the warranty liability. The $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c289" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:OtherExpenses" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23871">1.3</ix:nonFraction> million of income was
classified in Other income (expense), net within its consolidated statements of operations and comprehensive loss.</p>
<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">

</p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0"><i>Public, Private Placement, and Working Capital Warrants</i></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In conjunction with the Mergers, Complete Solaria,
as accounting acquirer, was deemed to assume <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c290" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23872">6,266,667</ix:nonFraction> warrants to purchase FACT Class A Ordinary Shares that were held by the sponsor
at an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c290" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23873">11.50</ix:nonFraction> (&#8220;Private Placement Warrants&#8221;) and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c291" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23874">8,625,000</ix:nonFraction> warrants to purchase FACT&#8217;s shareholders
FACT Class A Ordinary Shares at an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c291" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23875">11.50</ix:nonFraction> (&#8220;Public Warrants&#8221;). Subsequent to the Mergers, the Private Placement
Warrants and Public Warrants are exercisable for shares of Complete Solaria Common Stock and meet liability classification requirements
since the warrants may be required to be settled in cash under a tender offer. In addition, Private Placement Warrants are potentially
subject to a different settlement amount as a result of being held by the Sponsor which precludes the Private Placement Warrants from
being considered indexed to the entity&#8217;s own stock. Therefore, these warrants are classified as liabilities on the consolidated
balance sheets.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The fair values of the warrant liabilities were
$<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c292" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23876">1.5</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c293" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23877">0.3</ix:nonFraction> million as of December 29, 2024, and December 31, 2023, respectively. The Company recorded a $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c294" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23878">1.2</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c295" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23879">6.4</ix:nonFraction>
million increase in the fair value of these warrants for the fiscal years ended December 29, 2024 and December 31, 2023, respectively.
These changes were recorded in Other income (expense), net in the Company&#8217;s consolidated statements of operations and comprehensive
loss.</p></ix:continuation><div>
</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">

</p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"></p><div>

    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt">F-<!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->32<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
    </div><div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 12pt"><p style="margin: 0pt"></p></div><div>
    </div><!-- Field: /Page --><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><ix:continuation continuedAt="cslr_WarrantsTextBlock-c0_cont_3" id="cslr_WarrantsTextBlock-c0_cont_2"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">Additionally, at the closing of the Mergers, the Company issued <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c135" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="cslr:WorkingCapitalWarrants" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23880">716,668</ix:nonFraction>
Working Capital warrants, which have identical terms as the Private Placement Warrants to the sponsor in satisfaction of certain liabilities
of FACT. The warrants were fair valued at $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c296" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23881">0.3</ix:nonFraction> million upon the closing of the Mergers, which was recorded in warrant liability on the
Company&#8217;s consolidated balance sheets. As of December 29, 2024 and December 31, 2023, the Working Capital warrants had a fair value
of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c297" decimals="-4" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23882">0.08</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c298" decimals="-4" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23883">0.01</ix:nonFraction> million, respectively and the Company recorded the change in fair value of less than $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c298" decimals="-4" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:OtherExpenses" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23884">0.07</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c297" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:OtherExpenses" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23885">0.1</ix:nonFraction>
million in Other income (expense), net within the Company&#8217;s consolidated statements of operations and comprehensive loss in the
years ended December 29, 2024 and December 31, 2023, respectively.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><i>Previous Liability Classified Warrant Now Classified
as Equity</i></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0"><span style="text-decoration:underline">Carlyle Warrant </span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p>

<p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">In February 2022, as part of a debt financing from Carlyle (&#8220;CS
Solis Debt&#8221;) (refer to Note 15 &#8211; Borrowings and Derivative Liabilities), the Company issued a warrant to Carlyle to purchase
<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c299" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23886">2,886,952</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock (&#8220;Carlyle Warrant&#8221;). The warrant contained two tranches, the first of which was immediately
exercisable for <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c299" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationSharesAuthorizedUnderStockOptionPlansExercisePriceRangeNumberOfExercisableOptions" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23887">1,995,879</ix:nonFraction> shares of Legacy Complete Solaria common stock. The second tranche, which was determined to be a separate unit
of account, expired on December 31, 2022 prior to becoming exercisable. At issuance, the relative fair value of the warrant was determined
to be $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c300" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23888">3.4</ix:nonFraction> million using the Black-Scholes model and was initially recorded within additional paid-in capital as it met the conditions
for equity classification. The Carlyle Warrant has an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c103" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23889">0.01</ix:nonFraction> per share.</p>



<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In July 2023, and in connection with the closing
of the Mergers, the Carlyle debt and warrants were modified. Based on the exchange ratio included in the Mergers, the <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c301" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightOutstanding" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23890">1,995,879</ix:nonFraction> outstanding
warrants to purchase <span>Legacy Complete Solaria Common Stock</span> prior to modification were exchanged
into warrants to purchase <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c301" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23891">1,995,879</ix:nonFraction> shares of <span>Complete Solaria Common Stock</span>. As part of
the modification, the warrant, which expires on <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c302" format="ixt:date-monthname-day-year-en" name="cslr:WarrantExpiredTerm" id="ixv-23892">July 18, 2030</ix:nonNumeric>, provides Carlyle with the right to purchase shares of Complete Solaria
Common Stock based on (a) the greater of (i) <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c303" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23893">1,995,879</ix:nonFraction> shares and (ii) the number of shares equal to <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c82" decimals="5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="cslr:PercentageOfCommonStockSharesIssued" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23894"><ix:nonFraction contextRef="c304" decimals="5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="cslr:PercentageOfCommonStockSharesOutstanding" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23895"><ix:nonFraction contextRef="c82" decimals="5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="cslr:PercentageOfCommonStockSharesIssued" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23896">2.795</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction>% of Complete Solaria&#8217;s
issued and outstanding shares of common stock, on a fully-diluted basis; plus (b) on and after the date that is ten (10) days after the
date of the agreement, an additional <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c305" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23897">350,000</ix:nonFraction> shares; plus (c) on and after the date that is thirty (30) days after the date of the agreement,
if the original investment amount has not been repaid, an additional <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c306" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23898">150,000</ix:nonFraction> shares; plus (d) on and after the date that is ninety (90)
days after the date of the agreement, if the original investment amount has not been repaid, an additional <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c307" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23899">250,000</ix:nonFraction> shares, in each case,
of Complete Solaria Common Stock at a price of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c308" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:SharesIssuedPricePerShare" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23900">0.01</ix:nonFraction> per share. Of the additional warrants that become exercisable after the modification,
the tranches of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c309" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="cslr:WarrantVestingShares" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23901">350,000</ix:nonFraction> warrants vesting ten days after the date of the agreement and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c310" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="cslr:WarrantVestingShares" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23902">150,000</ix:nonFraction> warrants vesting thirty days after the date
of the agreement were exercisable as of October 31, 2023.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In December 2023, Carlyle was issued an additional
warrant to purchase an additional <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c150" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23903">2,190,604</ix:nonFraction> shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock related to an anti-dilution provision within the
CS Solis Debt that provides for such additional warrants under such circumstances as provided within the CS Solis Debt.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The modification of the warrant resulted in the reclassification of
previously equity-classified warrants to liability classification, which was accounted for in accordance with ASC 815 and ASC 718, Compensation
&#8211; Stock Compensation. The fair value of the warrant liability was determined based on its intrinsic value, given a nominal exercise
price. At issuance, the relative fair value of the warrant was determined to be $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c311" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23904">20.4</ix:nonFraction> million using the Black-Scholes model with the following
weighted average assumptions: expected term of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c312" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingMeasurementInput" scale="0" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23905"><ix:nonFraction contextRef="c312" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingMeasurementInput" scale="0" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23906">7</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction> years; expected volatility of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c313" decimals="1" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingMeasurementInput" scale="0" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23907">77.0</ix:nonFraction>%; risk-free interest rate of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c314" decimals="1" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingMeasurementInput" scale="0" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23908">3.9</ix:nonFraction>%; and <span style="-sec-ix-hidden: hidden-fact-242">no</span> dividend
yield. <p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In October 2021, the Company issued a warrant

2024. The fair value of the warrant was recorded within additional paid-in capital on the Company&#8217;s consolidated balance sheets.

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0"><span style="text-decoration:underline">July 2023 Common Stock Warrants</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In July 2023, the Company issued a warrant to
a third-party service provider to purchase <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c336" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23938">38,981</ix:nonFraction> shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock in exchange for services provided in obtaining
financing at the Closing of the Mergers. The warrant was immediately exercisable at a price of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c336" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23939">0.01</ix:nonFraction> per share and has an expiration date
of <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c0" name="cslr:WarrantExpirePeriod" id="ixv-23940">July 2028</ix:nonNumeric>. At issuance, the fair value of the warrant was determined to be $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c337" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23941">0.2</ix:nonFraction> million, based on the intrinsic value of the warrant
and the $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c338" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23942">0.01</ix:nonFraction> per share exercise price. As the warrant is accounted for as an equity issuance cost, the fair value of the warrant was
recorded within additional paid-in capital on the Company&#8217;s consolidated balance sheet. The warrant is not remeasured in future
periods as it meets the conditions for equity classification.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0"><span style="text-decoration:underline">Warrant Consideration</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In July 2023, in connection with the Mergers,
the Company issued <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c339" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23943">6,266,572</ix:nonFraction> warrants to purchase Complete Solaria Common Stock to holders of Legacy Complete Solaria Redeemable Convertible
Preferred Stock, Legacy Complete Solaria Common Stock. The exercise price of the common stock warrants is $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c339" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23944">11.50</ix:nonFraction> per share and the warrants
expire <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c339" format="ixt-sec:duryear" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingTerm" id="ixv-23945">10</ix:nonNumeric> years from the date of the Mergers. The warrant consideration was issued as part of the close of the Mergers and was recorded
within additional paid-in capital, net of the issuance costs of the Mergers. As of December&#160;29, 2024, this warrant remains outstanding.
The warrant is not remeasured in future periods as it meets the conditions for equity classification.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0"><span style="text-decoration:underline">Ayna Warrant</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">On June 17, 2024, a warrant to purchase shares of the Company&#8217;s
common stock (&#8220;Ayna Warrant&#8221;) was issued to Ayna.AI LLC (&#8220;Ayna&#8221;) for the purchase of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c340" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23946">6,000,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of the Company&#8217;s
common stock at an exercise price per share of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c340" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23947">0.01</ix:nonFraction>, subject to the provisions and upon the terms and conditions set forth in the Ayna
Warrant. The Ayna Warrant expires on June 17, 2029. The issuance of the Ayna Warrant by the Company to Ayna is in satisfaction of the
compensation for services provided to the Company by Ayna under the terms of a statement of work (&#8220;Ayna SOW&#8221;), signed May
21, 2024 (and effective as of March 12, 2024), as incorporated into a master services agreement dated March 12, 2024. Under the Ayna SOW,
Ayna provides services in connection with the anticipated return of the Company to cash-flow positive performance. The Ayna Warrant became
fully exercisable for the <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c341" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23948">6,000,000</ix:nonFraction> shares on September 9, 2024 and Anya exercised the Ayna Warrant in full for cash in January 2025.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In lieu of exercising the Ayna Warrant for cash,
Ayna may from time to time convert the Ayna Warrant, in whole or in part, into a number of shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock
determined by dividing (a) the aggregate fair market value of the shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock or other securities otherwise
issuable upon exercise of the Ayna Warrant minus the aggregate warrant price of such shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock by (b)
the fair market value (&#8220;Ayna Warrant FMV&#8221;) of one share of the Company&#8217;s common stock.&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">If the Company&#8217;s shares of common
stock are traded regularly in a public market, the Ayna Warrant FMV shall be the weighted average price for the 30 trading days ending
on the trading day immediately before Ayna delivers its notice of exercise to the Company. If the Company&#8217;s shares of common stock
are not regularly traded in a public market, the Company&#8217;s Board of Directors shall determine that the Ayna Warrant FMV in its reasonable
good faith judgment. The foregoing notwithstanding, if Ayna advises the Company&#8217;s Board of Directors in writing that Ayna disagrees
with such determination, then the Company and Ayna shall promptly agree upon a reputable investment banking firm or a third party independent
appraiser to undertake such valuation. If the valuation of such investment banking firm is greater than that determined by the Board of
Directors, then all fees and expenses of such investment banking firm shall be paid by the Company. In all other circumstances, such fee
and expenses shall be paid by Ayna.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">At issuance, the fair value of the Ayna Warrant
was determined to be $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c342" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23949">9.2</ix:nonFraction> million, based on the intrinsic value of the Ayna Warrant and the $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c343" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23950">0.01</ix:nonFraction> per share exercise price. As the Ayna
Warrant is accounted for as stock-based compensation under ASC 718, the Ayna Warrant is recorded within additional paid-in capital on
the consolidated balance sheets. The Ayna Warrant is not remeasured in future periods as it meets the conditions for equity classification.
As the Ayna statement of work period is different than the date of the warrant agreement, the differences in dates cause an accrued expense
for services rendered by Ayna. The Company recognized expenses incurred to date of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c342" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:InterestExpense" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23951">9.2</ix:nonFraction> million for the fiscal year ended December&#160;29,
2024, within General and administrative expenses on the Company&#8217;s consolidated statement of operations. The full amount of the
Ayna Warrant, $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c343" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:AdditionalPaidInCapital" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23952">9.2</ix:nonFraction> million, was recorded within additional paid-in-capital as of December&#160;29, 2024.</p></ix:continuation><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 7pt">&#160;</span></p><div>


</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"></p><div>

</div><ix:continuation id="cslr_WarrantsTextBlock-c0_cont_8"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="text-decoration:underline">Cantor Warrant</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 7pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In July 2024, the Company issued a warrant
(&#8220;Cantor Warrant&#8221;) to a third-party service provider to purchase <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c344" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" unitRef="shares" id="ixv-23953">3,066,141</ix:nonFraction> shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock in exchange
for services provided in the issuance of the July 2024 Notes (refer to <span>Note 15</span> &#8211; Borrowings
and Derivative Liabilities). The Cantor Warrant was immediately exercisable at a price of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c344" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23954">1.68</ix:nonFraction>&#160;per share and has an expiration date
in July 2029. At issuance, the fair value of the Cantor Warrant was determined to be $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c345" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23955">1.4</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million, of which $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c346" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentUnamortizedDiscount" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23956">0.9</ix:nonFraction> million was recorded
as a debt discount and $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c347" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ConvertibleNotesPayable" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23957">0.5</ix:nonFraction> million was attributable to the convertible notes issued in the Exchange Agreement and reduced the gain on
the troubled debt restructuring (refer to <span>Note 15</span> &#8211; Borrowings and Derivative Liabilities).
The fair value of this warrant was derived using the Black-Scholes model with the following assumptions: expected volatility of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c348" decimals="0" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingMeasurementInput" scale="0" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23958">55</ix:nonFraction>%; risk-free
interest rate of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c349" decimals="1" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingMeasurementInput" scale="0" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23959">4.2</ix:nonFraction>%; expected term of <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c344" format="ixt-sec:duryear" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingTerm" id="ixv-23960">5</ix:nonNumeric> years; and no dividend yield. The fair value of this warrant was recorded within additional
paid-in capital on the Company&#8217;s consolidated balance sheets and is not remeasured in future periods as it meets the conditions
for equity classification.</p></ix:continuation><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 7pt">&#160;</span></p><div>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 7pt"><i>&#160;</i></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><i>12% Senior Unsecured Convertible Notes</i></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 7pt">&#160;</span></p><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In July 2024, the Company issued $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c353" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:SeniorNotes" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23981">46.0</ix:nonFraction> million
of senior unsecured convertible notes (&#8220;July 2024 Notes&#8221;) to various lenders. Of the July 2024 Notes, $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c354" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:SeniorNotes" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23982">18.0</ix:nonFraction> million were issued
to a related party affiliated with the Company&#8217;s Chief Executive Officer and a director, Rodgers Massey Revocable Living Trust,
$<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c355" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentIssuedPrincipal" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23983">18.0</ix:nonFraction> million were issued in exchange for the cancellation of indebtedness as discussed below of which $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c356" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:SeniorNotes" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23984">10.0</ix:nonFraction> million were issued to Carlyle
which was also deemed to be a related party in the fiscal year ended December 29, 2024. The July 2024 Notes bear interest at <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c353" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateEffectivePercentage" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23985">12</ix:nonFraction>% per annum,
and the principal is payable in full at maturity on <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c355" format="ixt:date-monthname-day-year-en" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentMaturityDate" id="ixv-23986">July 1, 2029</ix:nonNumeric>. The interest is payable in cash on January 1 and July 1 of each year,
beginning on July 1, 2025. Upon default, principal and interest become immediately due and payable. The interest rate increases by <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c353" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-23987">3</ix:nonFraction>%
in the event of default. The July 2024 Notes are convertible into the Company&#8217;s common stock at the option of the holder at a conversion
rate of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c353" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentConvertibleConversionPrice1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-23988">1.68</ix:nonFraction> per share. Holders of July 2024 Notes may convert at any time. The July 2024 Notes may be declared due and payable at the
option of the holder upon event of default and upon a qualifying change of control event. The conversion option is required to be bifurcated
as a derivative liability, and the Company recorded a <span style="-sec-ix-hidden: hidden-fact-258">derivative liability</span> of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c353" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DerivativeLiabilities" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23989">28.7</ix:nonFraction> million on the issuance date with a corresponding
debt discount. In connection with the issuance of the July 2024 Notes, the Company issued the Cantor Warrant, as described in Note 14
&#8211; Warrants, to purchase shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock. At issuance, the Cantor Warrant had a fair value of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c355" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:FairValueAdjustmentOfWarrants" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23990">1.4</ix:nonFraction> million,
of which $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c355" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:AmortizationOfDebtDiscountPremium" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23991">0.9</ix:nonFraction> million was recorded as a debt discount, and $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c355" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:GainsLossesOnRestructuringOfDebt" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23992">0.5</ix:nonFraction> million was included in the calculation of the Company&#8217;s gain on
the troubled debt restructuring, as discussed above. As of December 29, 2024, the carrying amount of the convertible July 2024 Notes inclusive
of the fair value of the derivative liability was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c357" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ConvertibleNotesPayable" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23993">77.3</ix:nonFraction> million, which reflects a derivative liability of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c357" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DerivativeLiabilities" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23994">34.7</ix:nonFraction> million and convertible
notes of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c357" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ConvertibleDebt" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23995">70.4</ix:nonFraction> million, less an unamortized debt discount of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c357" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentUnamortizedDiscount" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23996">27.8</ix:nonFraction> million.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 7pt">&#160;</span></p>


<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">Interest expense recognized on the July 2024 Notes
was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c358" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseDebt" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23997">2.8</ix:nonFraction> million and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c9" decimals="0" format="ixt:fixed-zero" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseDebt" scale="0" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23998">zero</ix:nonFraction> in the fiscal years ended December 29, 2024 and December 31, 2023, respectively. Of the total interest expense,
related party interest expense was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c359" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseBorrowings" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-23999">1.7</ix:nonFraction> million and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c360" decimals="-6" format="ixt:fixed-zero" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseBorrowings" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24000">zero</ix:nonFraction> in the fiscal years ended December 29, 2024 and December 31, 2023, respectively.
Debt discount expense recognized on the July 2024 Notes was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c358" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseBorrowings" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24001">1.8</ix:nonFraction> million and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c360" decimals="-6" format="ixt:fixed-zero" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseBorrowings" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24002">zero</ix:nonFraction> in the fiscal years ended December 29, 2024 and December
31, 2023, respectively. Of the total debt discount expense, related party expense was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c0" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:CostsAndExpensesRelatedParty" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24003">1.1</ix:nonFraction> million and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c9" decimals="0" format="ixt:fixed-zero" name="us-gaap:CostsAndExpensesRelatedParty" scale="0" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24004">zero</ix:nonFraction> in the fiscal years ended
December 29, 2024, and December 31, 2023, respectively. There are no financial covenants. The July 2024 Notes are not in default. However,
due to the Company&#8217;s delayed filing of its Form 10K for the year ended December 29, 2024, the Company will be required to pay incremental
default interest of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c0" decimals="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="cslr:IncrementalDefaultInterest" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-24005">0.5</ix:nonFraction>% beginning April 16, 2025, which will cease upon the Company&#8217;s filing of its Form 10K.</p></ix:nonNumeric><div>

million of contingent interest which is payable upon default is included in the July 2024 Notes and July 2024 Notes - related parties,

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt"><i>7% Senior Unsecured Convertible Notes</i></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt"><i>&#160;</i></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In September 2024, the Company issued $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c363" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:SeniorNotes" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24012">66.8</ix:nonFraction> million of senior unsecured
convertible notes to various lenders (the &#8220;September 2024 Notes&#8221;), $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c364" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:SeniorNotes" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24013">4.0</ix:nonFraction> million of which were issued to Rodgers Family Freedom
and Free Markets Charitable Trust (&#8220;Massey Charitable Trust&#8221;), a related party and $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c365" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:SeniorNotes" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24014">4.0</ix:nonFraction> million were issued to Rodgers Massey
Revocable Living Trust (collectively with Massey Charitable Trust, &#8220;Massey Trusts&#8221;), also a related party. The September 2024
Notes bear interest at <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c363" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateEffectivePercentage" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-24015">7</ix:nonFraction>% per annum, and the principal is payable in full at maturity on <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c366" format="ixt:date-monthname-day-year-en" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentMaturityDate" id="ixv-24016">July 1, 2029</ix:nonNumeric>. The interest is payable in cash
on January 1 and July 1 of each year, beginning on July 1, 2025. Upon default, principal and interest become immediately due and payable.
The September 2024 Notes are convertible into shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock at the option of the holder at a conversion rate
of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c367" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentConvertibleConversionPrice1" scale="0" unitRef="usdPershares" id="ixv-24017">2.14</ix:nonFraction> per common share. Holders of September 2024 Notes may convert at any time. The September 2024 Notes may be declared due and payable
at the option of the holder upon event of default and upon a qualifying change of control event. The conversion option is required to
be bifurcated as a derivative liability, and the Company recorded a derivative liability of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c363" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DerivativeLiabilities" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24018">91.5</ix:nonFraction> million on the issuance date. As the
fair value of the derivative liability exceeds the proceeds received, the Company recorded a corresponding financing loss of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c366" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DerivativeGainLossOnDerivativeNet" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24019">24.7</ix:nonFraction> million
and debt discount for $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c366" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:AmortizationOfDebtDiscountPremium" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24020">66.8</ix:nonFraction> million as of the issuance date. In December 2024, the Company issued an additional $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c368" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:SeniorNotes" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24021">13.0</ix:nonFraction> million of September
2024 Notes for cash. The Company recognized a $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c369" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:AmortizationOfDebtDiscountPremium" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24022">10.9</ix:nonFraction> million debt discount in connection with these additional proceeds.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">
&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">As of December 29, 2024, the carrying amount of
the September 2024 Notes inclusive of the fair value of the derivative liability was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c368" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ConvertibleDebt" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24023">68.5</ix:nonFraction> million, which reflects a derivative liability
of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c368" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DerivativeLiabilities" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24024">62.4</ix:nonFraction> million and convertible notes of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c368" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:ConvertibleNotesPayable" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24025">79.8</ix:nonFraction> million, less an unamortized debt discount of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c368" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentUnamortizedDiscount" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24026">73.7</ix:nonFraction> million.</p>
<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>


<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">Interest expense recognized on the September 2024
Notes was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c370" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseBorrowings" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24027">1.4</ix:nonFraction> million and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c371" decimals="-6" format="ixt:fixed-zero" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseBorrowings" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24028">zero</ix:nonFraction> in the fiscal years ended December 29, 2024 and December 31, 2023, respectively. Of the total interest
expense, related party interest expense was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c372" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseBorrowings" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24029">0.2</ix:nonFraction> million and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c373" decimals="-6" format="ixt:fixed-zero" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseBorrowings" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24030">zero</ix:nonFraction> in the fiscal years ended December 29, 2024 and 2023, respectively.
Debt discount expense recognized on the September 2024 Notes was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c374" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseBorrowings" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24031">4.0</ix:nonFraction> million and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c375" decimals="0" format="ixt:fixed-zero" name="us-gaap:InterestExpenseBorrowings" scale="0" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24032">zero</ix:nonFraction> in the fiscal years ended December 29, 2024 and
December 31, 2023, respectively. Of the total debt discount expense, related party expense was $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c374" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:CostsAndExpensesRelatedParty" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24033">0.5</ix:nonFraction> million and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c375" decimals="0" format="ixt:fixed-zero" name="us-gaap:CostsAndExpensesRelatedParty" scale="0" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24034">zero</ix:nonFraction> in the fiscal years
ended December 29, 2024, and December 31, 2023, respectively. There are no financial covenants. The September 2024 Notes are not in default.
However, due to the Company&#8217;s delayed filing of its Form 10K for the year ended December 29, 2024, the Company will be required
to pay incremental default interest of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c0" decimals="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="cslr:IncrementalDefaultInterest" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-24035">0.5</ix:nonFraction>% beginning April 16, 2025, which will cease upon the Company&#8217;s filing of its Form 10K.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The effective interest rate is <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c376" decimals="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateEffectivePercentage" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-24036">27.6</ix:nonFraction>% and <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c6" decimals="3" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateEffectivePercentage" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-24037">47.3</ix:nonFraction>%
on the September 2024 Notes&#8217; principal amounts of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c0" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentConvertibleIfConvertedValueInExcessOfPrincipal" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24038">66.8</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c374" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentConvertibleIfConvertedValueInExcessOfPrincipal" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24039">13.0</ix:nonFraction> million, respectively.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><i>Exchange Agreement </i></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">On July 1, 2024, the Company entered into an Exchange
Agreement (the &#8220;Exchange Agreement&#8221;) with Carlyle and Kline Hill (as defined below) providing for:</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;">
  <tr style="vertical-align: top">
    <td style="width: 0.25in">&#160;</td>
    <td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">(i)</span></td>
    <td style="text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">the cancellation of all indebtedness, inclusive of the CS Solis Debt, owed to Carlyle by the Company, termination of all debt instruments by and between the Company and Carlyle (through the transfer of Carlyle&#8217;s interest in CS Solis, LLC, to the Company), and the satisfaction of all obligations owed to Carlyle by the Company under the terminated debt instruments;</span></td></tr>
</table>

<p style="margin: 0">&#160;</p>

<table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;"> <tr style="vertical-align: top"> <td style="width: 0.25in">&#160;</td> <td style="text-align: justify; width: 0.25in"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">(ii)</span></td> <td style="text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">the issuance of a note for the principal amount of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c377" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentFaceAmount" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24040">10.0</ix:nonFraction> million to Carlyle as part of the July 2024 Notes;</span></td></tr>
</table>

<p style="margin: 0">&#160;</p>

<table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;">
  <tr style="vertical-align: top">
    <td style="width: 0.25in">&#160;</td>

    <td style="text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">the cancellation of all indebtedness owed to Kline Hill Partners Fund LP, Kline Hill Partners IV SPV LLC, and Kline Hill Partners Opportunity IV SPV, LLC (collectively &#8220;Kline Hill&#8221;). by the Company, termination of all debt instruments by and between the Company and Kline Hill, including the 2018 Bridge Notes, the revolving loan and the secured credit facility, and the satisfaction of all obligations owed to Kline Hill by the Company under the terminated debt instruments;</span></td></tr>
</table>

<p style="margin: 0">&#160;</p>

<table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; border-spacing: 0px;"> <tr style="vertical-align: top"> <td style="width: 0.25in">&#160;</td> <td style="text-align: justify; width: 0.25in"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">(iv)</span></td> <td style="text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">the issuance of a note for the principal amount of $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c378" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentFaceAmount" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24041">8.0</ix:nonFraction> million to Kline Hill as part of the July 2024 Notes; and</span></td></tr>
</table>

<p style="margin: 0">&#160;</p>

per share, of the Company (the &#8220;Common Stock&#8221;) to Kline Hill (the &#8220;Shares&#8221;).</span></td></tr> </table>
<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><i>&#160;</i></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0"><i>2018 Bridge Notes</i></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">In 2018, Solaria issued senior subordinated convertible
secured notes (&#8220;2018 Notes&#8221;) totaling approximately $<ix:nonFraction contextRef="c380" decimals="-5" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:SecuredDebt" scale="6" unitRef="usd" id="ixv-24044">3.4</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million in exchange for cash. The 2018 Notes were secured by
substantially all of the assets of Solaria, bore interest at the rate of <ix:nonFraction contextRef="c380" decimals="2" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateEffectivePercentage" scale="-2" unitRef="pure" id="ixv-24045">8</ix:nonFraction>% per annum, and the investors were entitled to receive twice
the face value of the 2018 Notes at maturity. In connection with an amendment in 2021 to extend the maturity date of the 2018 Notes, Solaria
issued warrants to purchase shares of Series E-1 redeemable convertible preferred stock of Solaria. The warrants were exercisable immediately
in whole or in part at and expire on December 13, 2031. As part of the Business Combination with Complete Solar, the outstanding warrants
issued were assumed by the parent company, Complete Solaria.</p></ix:continuation><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"></p><div>

    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt">F-<!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->37<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
