</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-indent: 0.5in; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">Biographical information for Mr.&#160;Rodgers
is included with the director biographies above.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><b><i>Daniel Foley</i></b>.<b><i>&#160;</i></b>Daniel
Foley,&#160;48, has served as the Chief Financial Officer of Complete Solaria since June&#160;2024. From June&#160;2021 to December&#160;2023,
Mr.&#160;Foley served as the Chief Financial Officer for Common Citizen. From April&#160;2021 to June&#160;2021, Mr.&#160;Foley served
as the Senior Vice President and Treasurer for TerrAscend. From January&#160;2018 to April&#160;2021, Mr.&#160;Foley served as the Vice
President of Corporate Finance, Treasury&#160;&amp; Investor Relations at Curaleaf. Prior to that, Mr.&#160;Foley held senior positions
in Corporate Finance and Investor Relations for Station Casinos and MGM MIRAGE.&#160;Previous experience includes working as an Investment
Analyst at Wall Street Associates and as Vice President of Finance at New Cotai Holdings. Foley began his career as a Senior Associate
in Gaming, Lodging&#160;&amp; Leisure Equity Research at Bear Stearns. Mr.&#160;Foley brings over 25&#160;years of capital markets and
finance experience to Complete Solaria, as well as a track record of driving strong financial results, instilling financial and operational
discipline, and demonstrating inspirational leadership. Mr.&#160;Foley holds an M.B.A. from the University of Southern California and
a Bachelor of Science in economics from the University of Utah.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Director Independence</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">As required under Nasdaq listing standards, a
majority of the members of a listed company&#8217;s Board of Directors must qualify as &#8220;independent,&#8221; as affirmatively determined
by the Board of Directors. In addition, Nasdaq listing standards require that, subject to specified exceptions, each member of a listed
company&#8217;s Audit Committee, Compensation Committee, and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee be &#8220;independent.&#8221;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Based on information provided by each director
concerning her or his background, employment and affiliations, the Board of Directors affirmatively determined that none of our directors&#160;&#8212;&#160;other
than Thurman J.&#160;Rodgers, Antonio Alvarez, William Anderson, and Chris Lundell&#160;&#8212;&#160;has any relationships that would
interfere with the exercise of independent judgment in carrying out the responsibilities of a director and that each of Messrs. Gishen,
Maier, Pasek, McCranie, Thiam and Whatley, representing a majority of Complete Solaria&#8217;s incumbent directors, is &#8220;independent&#8221;
as that term is defined under the Nasdaq listing standards.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">There are no family relationships among any of
our directors or executive officers.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Role of the Board of Directors in Risk Oversight</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">One of the key functions of the Board of Directors
is the informed oversight of Complete Solaria&#8217;s risk management process. The Board of Directors does not anticipate having a standing
risk management committee, but rather anticipates administering this oversight function directly through the Board of Directors as a whole,
as well as through various standing committees of the Board of Directors that address risks inherent in their respective areas of oversight.
In particular, the Board of Directors is responsible for monitoring and assessing strategic risk exposure, and Complete Solaria&#8217;s
Audit Committee is responsible for considering and discussing Complete Solaria&#8217;s major financial risk exposures and the steps its
management will take to monitor and control such exposures, including guidelines and policies to govern the process by which risk assessment
and management is undertaken. The Audit Committee monitors compliance with legal and regulatory requirements. The Compensation Committee
assesses and monitors whether Complete Solaria&#8217;s compensation plans, policies and programs comply with applicable legal and regulatory
requirements.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"></p><div>

</div><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 115; Value: 52 --><div>
    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt"><!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->58<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
    </div><div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 12pt"><p style="margin: 0pt"></p></div><div>
    </div><!-- Field: /Page --><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Board Committees</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Our Board of Directors has formed an Audit Committee,
Compensation Committee, and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee. The Board of Directors may from time to time establish other
committees.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Our Chief Executive Officer, Chief Financial Officer
and other executive officers regularly report to the non-executive directors and each standing committee to ensure effective and efficient
oversight of its activities and to assist in proper risk management and the ongoing evaluation of management controls.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>Audit Committee</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">The Audit Committee consists of Ronald Pasek,
who serves as the chairperson, Adam Gishen and Lothar Maier. Each member of the Audit Committee qualifies as an independent director under
the Nasdaq corporate governance standards and the independence requirements of Rule&#160;10A-3 under the Exchange&#160;Act. Our Board
of Directors has determined that Ronald Pasek qualifies as an &#8220;audit committee financial expert&#8221; as such term is defined in
Item&#160;407(d)(5)&#160;of Regulation&#160;S-K and possesses the requisite financial expertise required under the applicable requirements
of Nasdaq. As discussed above, our Board of Directors has also determined that Ronald Pasek is an independent director.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">The responsibilities of the Audit Committee include,
among other things:</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">helping the Board of Directors oversee corporate accounting
and financial reporting processes;</td>
</tr></table><div>

independent registered public accounting firm;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">discussing the scope and results of the audit with the independent
registered public accounting firm, and reviewing, with management and the independent accountants, Complete Solaria&#8217;s interim and
year-end operating results;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">developing procedures for employees to submit concerns anonymously
about questionable accounting or audit matters;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

risk management;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">reviewing related party transactions;</td>
</tr></table><div>

public accounting firm at least annually, that describes Complete Solaria&#8217;s internal quality-control procedures, any material issues

</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">approving (or, as permitted, pre-approving) all audit and
all permissible non-audit service to be performed by the independent registered public accounting firm.</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">The Board of Directors adopted a written charter
of the Audit Committee which is available on Complete Solaria&#8217;s website.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"></p><div>

    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt"><!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->59<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
    </div><div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 12pt"><p style="margin: 0pt"></p></div><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>Compensation Committee</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">The Compensation Committee consists of Daniel
McCranie, who serves as the chairperson, Devin Whatley and Tidjane Thiam. The Board of Directors has determined that each current member
of the Compensation Committee is independent. The Board of Directors has also determined that each current committee member and a former
member of the Compensation Committee&#160;&#8212;&#160;Antonio R.&#160;Alvarez&#160;&#8212;&#160;is a &#8220;non-employee director&#8221;
as defined in Rule&#160;16b-3 promulgated under the Exchange&#160;Act. Mr.&#160;Alvarez resigned from the Compensation Committee during
April&#160;2025. While Mr.&#160;Alvarez is not an independent director, Section&#160;5605(d)(2)(B)&#160;of the Nasdaq listing standards
nonetheless permitted the appointment of a non-independent director to the compensation committee if the board of directors, under exceptional
and limited circumstances, determines that the non-independent director&#8217;s membership is required by the best interests of the Company
and its stockholders. Based on Mr.&#160;Alvarez&#8217;s extensive experience with Complete Solaria and familiarity with the industry,
the Board of Directors previously concluded that Mr.&#160;Alvarez&#8217;s appointment to, and membership on, the Compensation Committee
prior to Mr.&#160;Alvarez&#8217;s resignation from the Compensation Committee was in the best interests of Complete Solaria and its stockholders.
Further, a majority of the members of the Compensation Committee are independent directors.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">The responsibilities of the Compensation Committee
are:</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">reviewing and approving, or recommending that the Board of
Directors approve, the compensation of Complete Solaria&#8217;s executive officers and senior management;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">reviewing and recommending to the Board of Directors the compensation
of Complete Solaria&#8217;s directors;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">reviewing and approving, or recommending that the Board of
Directors approve, the terms of compensatory arrangements with Complete Solaria&#8217;s executive;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">administering Complete Solaria&#8217;s stock and equity incentive
plans;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">selecting independent compensation consultants and assessing
whether there are any conflicts of interest with any of the committee&#8217;s compensation advisors;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">reviewing, approving, amending and terminating, or recommending
that the Board of Directors approve, amend or terminate, incentive compensation and equity plans, severance agreements, change-of-control
protections and any other compensatory arrangements for Complete Solaria&#8217;s executive officers and other senior management, as appropriate;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">reviewing and establishing general policies relating to compensation
and benefits of Complete Solaria&#8217;s employees; and</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">reviewing Complete Solaria&#8217;s overall compensation.</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-indent: 0.5in; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The Board of Directors adopted a written charter
for the Compensation Committee which is available on Complete Solaria&#8217;s website.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-indent: 0.5in; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee
consists of Ronald Pasek, who serves as the chairperson, and Adam Gishen. The responsibilities of the Nominating and Corporate Governance
Committee are:</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">identifying, evaluating and selecting, or recommending that
the Board of Directors approve, nominees for election to the Board of Directors;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">evaluating the performance of the Board of Directors and of
individual directors;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">evaluating the adequacy of Complete Solaria&#8217;s corporate
governance practices and reporting;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">reviewing management succession plans; and</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">developing and making recommendations to the Board of Directors
regarding corporate governance guidelines and matters.</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"></p><div>

</div><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 117; Value: 52 --><div>
    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt"><!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->60<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
    </div><div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 12pt"><p style="margin: 0pt"></p></div><div>
    </div><!-- Field: /Page --><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-indent: 0.5in; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">The Board of Directors adopted a written charter
of the Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee which is available on Complete Solaria&#8217;s website.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">The Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee
evaluates all candidates for director thoroughly, whether they are recommended by the management team, stockholders or third parties,
in accordance with the needs of the Board of Directors and the qualifications of the candidate.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Code of Ethical Business Conduct</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Complete Solaria has adopted a code of ethical
business conduct that applies to all of its directors, officers and employees, including its principal executive officer, principal financial
officer and principal accounting officer, and is available on Complete Solaria&#8217;s website at https://investors.completesolar.com/corporate-governance.
Complete Solaria&#8217;s code of business conduct is a &#8220;code of ethics,&#8221; as defined in Item&#160;406(b)&#160;of Regulation&#160;S-K.
If we grant any waiver from a provision of the code of ethical business conduct to any executive officer or director, we will disclose
it on our website.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Compensation Committee Interlocks and Insider
Participation</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">No member of the Compensation Committee was at
any time during 2024, or at any other time, one of Complete Solaria&#8217;s officers or employees, except Mr.&#160;Alvarez who served
as Complete Solaria&#8217;s president until March&#160;2023. None of Complete Solaria&#8217;s executive officers has served as a director
or member of a compensation committee (or other committee serving an equivalent function) of any entity, one of whose executive officers
served as a director of our Board of Directors or member of the Compensation Committee.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Insider Trading Policy</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">We have adopted an insider trading policy that
applies to all of our directors and to certain of our employees and consultants. This policy prohibits engaging in short sales, transactions
in put or call options, hedging transactions, or other inherently speculative transactions with respect to our securities or derivative
securities at any time. We believe our insider trading policy is reasonably designed to promote compliance with insider trading laws,
rules and regulations, as well as applicable Nasdaq listing standards. A copy of our insider trading policy is included as Exhibit 19.1
to this Annual Report on Form&#160;10-K.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Delinquent Section&#160;16(a)&#160;Reports</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Based solely on our review of such forms furnished
to the Company and written representations from certain reporting persons, we believe that all filing requirements applicable to our executive
officers, directors and greater than 10% beneficial owners were timely made during fiscal 2024, with the exception of the following late
Form&#160;4s: (1)&#160;Mr.&#160;Rodgers was late filing Form&#160;4s relating to the issuance of the First SAFE (as defined below), the
issuance of the Second SAFE (as defined below), the amendment of the First SAFE and Second SAFE and related issuance of the Amendment
Shares (as defined below), the issuance of the Rodgers Group SAFE, and the sale and issuance of the July&#160;2024 Notes (as defined below);
(2)&#160;Tidjane Thiam was late filing a Form 4 relating to the sale of shares by him on December&#160;17, 2025; (3)&#160;William Anderson
was late filing Forms 4 in connection with the exercise of the restricted stock unit originally issued to him on August 30, 2024 and with
respect to stock option awards granted to him on February 1, 2024 and April 10, 2024; (4) Chris Lundell was late filing a Form 4 in connection
with the issuance of a stock option award granted on April 29, 2024; and (5) Daniel Foley was late filing a Form 4 in connection with
the issuance of a stock option award granted on June 11, 2024.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><div><a id="m_002"></a></div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>ITEM 11. EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>FACT</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>Employment Agreements</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Prior to the Closing of the Business Combination,
FACT did not enter into any employment agreements with its executive officers and did not make any agreements to provide benefits upon
termination of employment.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"></p><div>

</div><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 118; Value: 52 --><div>
    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt"><!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->61<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
    </div><div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 12pt"><p style="margin: 0pt"></p></div><div>
    </div><!-- Field: /Page --><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b><i>Executive Officers and Director Compensation</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">No FACT executive officers or directors received
any cash compensation for services rendered to FACT prior to the Business Combination.&#160;FACT paid its sponsor or an affiliate thereof
up to $10,000 per month for office space, utilities, secretarial and administrative support services provided to members of our management
team and other expenses and obligations of our sponsor. Executive officers and directors, or any of their respective affiliates were reimbursed
for any out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with activities on FACT&#8217;s behalf such as identifying potential target businesses
and performing due diligence on suitable business combinations.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Complete Solaria</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Complete Solaria has opted to comply with the
executive compensation disclosure rules applicable to emerging growth companies. The scaled down disclosure rules are those applicable
to &#8220;smaller reporting companies,&#8221; as such term is defined in the rules promulgated under the Securities Act&#160;of&#160;1933,
as amended (the &#8220;<b><i>Securities Act</i></b>&#8221;), which require compensation disclosure for all individuals serving as Complete
Solaria&#8217;s principal executive officer during 2024, the two most highly compensated executive officers of Complete Solaria, other
than the principal executive officer, whose total compensation for 2024 exceeded $100,000 and who were serving as executive officers as
of December&#160;29, 2024, and up to two additional individuals for whom disclosure under the applicable rules would have been provided
but for the fact that such individuals were not serving as executive officers at the end of 2024. Complete Solaria refers to these individuals
as &#8220;named executive officers.&#8221; For 2024, Complete Solaria&#8217;s named executive officers were:</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">Thurman J. (T.J.) Rodgers, Complete Solaria&#8217;s Chief
Executive Officer and Executive Chairman;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">Chris Lundell, Complete Solaria&#8217;s former Chief Executive
Officer;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">Daniel Foley, Complete Solaria&#8217;s Chief Financial Officer;
and</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">Brian Wuebbels, Complete Solaria&#8217;s former Chief Financial
Officer and former Chief Operations Officer.</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Complete Solaria believes its compensation program
should promote the success of the Company and align executive incentives with the long-term interests of its stockholders. Complete Solaria&#8217;s
current compensation programs reflect its startup origins in that they consist primarily of salary and equity-based awards. As Complete
Solaria&#8217;s needs evolve, Complete Solaria intends to continue to evaluate its philosophy and compensation programs as circumstances
require.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">During 2024, Mr.&#160;Rodgers did not receive
any separate compensation in his role as our Chief Executive Officer.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Summary Compensation Table</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">The following table shows information regarding
the compensation of Complete Solaria&#8217;s named executive officers for services performed in the fiscal year ended December&#160;29,
2024 and in the fiscal year ended December&#160;31, 2023.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

on the one-year anniversary of the grant date.</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">

on 7/1/2025, thereafter the remaining 80% of shares underlying the option award vest in 48 equal monthly installments.</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">

on 5/19/2024.</td>

option award vest in 60 equal monthly installments, beginning 6/19/2024.</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">

</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Employment Arrangements with Named Executive
Officers</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Each of Complete Solaria&#8217;s named executive
officers is or was an at-will employee.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 24pt; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 0.25in; text-align: justify"><b><i>Thurman J.&#160;Rodgers</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Mr.&#160;Rodgers is not currently a party to any
employment agreement or other understanding with respect to compensation as our Chief Executive Officer. Mr.&#160;Rodgers did not receive
separate compensation in his role as Chief Executive Officer during 2024.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 24pt; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 0.25in; text-align: justify"><b><i>Daniel Foley</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">On Jun 7, 2024, we entered into an executive employment
agreement with Mr.&#160;Foley, which became effective on July&#160;1, 2024 (the &#8220;<b><i>Foley Agreement</i></b>&#8221;). Pursuant
to the Foley Agreement. Mr.&#160;Foley is entitled to a base salary of $275,000 per year, and he will be eligible for an annual bonus
of 50% of his gross salary. Mr.&#160;Foley also received an option to purchase 500,000 shares of our common stock, subject to a five-year
vesting schedule. The Foley Agreement also provides that if Mr.&#160;Foley&#8217;s employment is terminated for any reason other than
cause (as defined in the Foley Agreement), death or disability, or if he resigns for good reason (as defined in the Foley Agreement),
and provided that in either case such termination constitutes a separation from service (as defined in the Foley Agreement), then subject
to Mr.&#160;Foley executing a release agreement in Complete Solaria&#8217;s favor, and continuing to comply with all of his obligations
to Complete Solaria and its affiliates, he will receive the following benefits: (a)&#160;payment of Mr.&#160;Foley&#8217;s earned but
unpaid base salary; (b)&#160;payment of such officer of any unpaid bonus, with respect to the fiscal year immediately preceding the fiscal
year in which such termination or such resignation occurs; (c)&#160;payment of any vested benefits to which he may be entitled under any
applicable plans and programs of the Company; (d)&#160;a severance payment equal to six&#160;months of Mr.&#160;Foley&#8217;s then base
salary plus a pro rata portion of Mr.&#160;Foley bonus with respect to the fiscal year in which such termination or such resignation occurs.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 24pt; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 24pt; text-align: justify"></p><div>

</div><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 120; Value: 52 --><div>
    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt"><!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->63<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
    </div><div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 12pt"><p style="margin: 0pt"></p></div><div>
    </div><!-- Field: /Page --><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 24pt; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 0.25in; text-align: justify"><b><i>Chris Lundell</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">On December&#160;7, 2023, we entered into an executive
employment with Chris Lundell to serve as our Chief Executive Officer (the &#8220;<b><i>Lundell Agreement</i></b>&#8221;). Pursuant to
the Lundell Agreement, Mr.&#160;Lundell was entitled to a base salary of $450,000 per year, and he was eligible for an annual bonus of
75% of his gross salary. Mr.&#160;Lundell also was previously granted an option to purchase 3,000,000 shares of our Common Stock. The
Lundell Agreement provided that if Mr.&#160;Lundell&#8217;s employment was terminated for any reason other than cause (as defined in the
Lundell Agreement), death or disability, or if he resigned for good reason (as defined in the Lundell Agreement), and provided that in
either case such termination constitutes a separation from service (as defined in the Lundell Agreement) and the separation is not on
or within 12&#160;months following a change of control, then subject to his executing a release agreement in our favor, and continuing
to comply with all of his obligations to Complete Solaria and our affiliates, he would receive the following benefits: (a)&#160;payment
of Mr.&#160;Lundell&#8217;s earned but unpaid base salary; (b)&#160;payment of any unpaid bonus, with respect to the fiscal year immediately
preceding the fiscal year in which such termination or such resignation occurs; (c)&#160;payment of any vested benefits to which he may
be entitled under any applicable plans and programs of Complete Solaria; (d)&#160;a severance payment equal to six&#160;months of Mr.&#160;Lundell&#8217;s
then-base salary plus a pro rata portion of Mr.&#160;Lundell&#8217;s bonus with respect to the fiscal year in which such termination or
such resignation occurs; (e)&#160;if he timely and properly elects to continue group health care coverage under the Consolidated Omnibus
Budget Reconciliation Act&#160;of&#160;1985 (&#8220;<b><i>COBRA</i></b>&#8221;), payment of Mr.&#160;Lundell&#8217;s COBRA premium expenses
until the earliest of (i)&#160;the six-month anniversary of the termination date; (ii)&#160;the date he is no longer eligible to receive
COBRA continuation coverage; and (iii)&#160;the date on which he becomes eligible to receive substantially similar coverage from another
employer; and (f)&#160;the applicable post-termination exercised period for any vested options will extend to the earlier of (i)&#160;the
six-month anniversary of the termination date, (ii)&#160;the expiration date of the option or (iii)&#160;earlier termination upon a corporate
transaction.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">On April&#160;24, 2024 (the &#8220;<b><i>Lundell
Separation Date</i></b>&#8221;), Mr.&#160;Lundell stepped down as Chief Executive Officer. Pursuant to Mr.&#160;Lundell&#8217;s Separation
Agreement, dated May&#160;19, 2024, he received:</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">cash severance in an amount equal to six&#160;months of his
base salary in effect as of the Lundell Separation Date, payable in installments beginning on the date that is the 60<sup>th</sup>&#160;day
following the Lundell Separation Date;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">reimbursement of premiums, if any, for up to twelve (12)&#160;months
following the Lundell Separation Date, provided proof of enrollment, for group healthcare coverage under COBRA;</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">350,000 stock options with 100,000 options vesting immediately,
and with the remaining 250,000 stock options vesting monthly at 1/60<sup>th</sup>&#160;of the total value over five (5)&#160;years contingent
upon continuing support to the Company; and</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 48pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: -24pt">&#160;</p><div>

</div><table cellpadding="0" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; width: 100%; border-spacing: 0px;"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: justify">
<td style="width: 0.5in"/><td style="width: 0.25in; text-align: left">&#9679;</td><td style="text-align: justify">retention of the 94,452 options that were granted to Mr.&#160;Lundell
as a board member on December&#160;3, 2023.</td>
</tr></table><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 24pt; text-align: justify"><b><i>&#160;</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0 0pt 0.25in; text-align: justify"><b><i>Brian Wuebbels</i></b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">On April&#160;24, 2024, we entered into an executive
employment agreement (the &#8220;<b><i>Wuebbels Agreement</i></b>&#8221;) with Brian Wuebbels to serve as Chief Operations Officer. Mr.&#160;Wuebbels
was promoted from his position as Chief Financial Officer of the Company to Chief Operations Officer as of such date.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Pursuant to the Wuebbels Agreement, Mr.&#160;Wuebbels
was entitled to a base salary of $330,000 per year, and he was eligible for an annual bonus of 50% of his gross salary. Mr.&#160;Wuebbels
also previously was granted an option to purchase 750,000 shares of our Common Stock. The Wuebbels Agreement also provides that if Mr.&#160;Wuebbels&#8217;s
employment was terminated for any reason other than cause (as defined in the Wuebbels Agreement), death or disability, or if such officer
resigns for good reason (as defined in the Wuebbels Agreement), and provided that in either case such termination constitutes separation
from service (as defined in the Wuebbels Agreement) and the separation is not on or within 12&#160;months following a change of control,
then subject to Mr.&#160;Wuebbels executing a release agreement in Complete Solaria&#8217;s favor, and continuing to comply with all of
his obligations to Complete Solaria and its affiliates, he will receive the following benefits: (a)&#160;payment of Mr.&#160;Wuebbels
earned but unpaid base salary; (b)&#160;payment to Mr.&#160;Wuebbels of any unpaid bonus, with respect to the fiscal year immediately
preceding the fiscal year in which such termination or such resignation occurs; (c)&#160;payment to Mr.&#160;Wuebbels of any vested benefits
to which he may be entitled under any applicable plans and programs of the Company; (d)&#160;a severance payment equal to six&#160;months
of Mr.&#160;Wuebbels then base salary plus a pro rata portion of Mr.&#160;Wuebbels bonus with respect to the fiscal year in which such
termination or such resignation occurs; (e)&#160;if Mr.&#160;Wuebbels timely and properly elects to continue group health care coverage
under COBRA, payment of Mr.&#160;Wuebbels&#8217;s COBRA premium expenses until the earliest of (i)&#160;the three-month anniversary of
the termination date; (ii)&#160;the date Mr.&#160;Wuebbels is no longer eligible to receive COBRA continuation coverage; and (iii)&#160;the
date on which Mr.&#160;Wuebbels becomes eligible to receive substantially similar coverage from another employer; and (f)&#160;the applicable
post-termination exercised period for any vested options will extend to the earlier of (i)&#160;the six-month anniversary of the termination
date, (ii)&#160;the expiration date of the option or (iii)&#160;earlier termination upon corporate transaction.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Effective August&#160;16, 2024, Brian Wuebbels
resigned as our Chief Operations Officer.&#160;On June&#160;30, 2024, we&#160;entered into an&#160;employment extension agreement (the
&#8220;<b><i>Extension Agreement</i></b>&#8221;) with Mr.&#160;Wuebbels. Pursuant to the Extension Agreement, Mr.&#160;Wuebbels&#8217;
health benefits continued through August&#160;31, 2024, and Mr.&#160;Wuebbels received accelerated vesting of 208,115 of the 750,000 options
that he was granted in April&#160;2024.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"></p><div>

</div><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 121; Value: 52 --><div>
    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt"><!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->64<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
    </div><div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 12pt"><p style="margin: 0pt"></p></div><div>
    </div><!-- Field: /Page --><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Base Salary</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Base salaries are intended to provide a level
of compensation sufficient to attract and retain an effective management team, when considered in combination with the other components
of the executive compensation program. In general, Complete Solaria seeks to provide a base salary level designed to reflect each executive
officer&#8217;s scope of responsibility and accountability.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Bonuses</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Beginning January&#160;1, 2024, each of our named
executive officers (other than Mr.&#160;Rodgers) was eligible for an annual bonus of 50% of such officer&#8217;s annual gross salary,
based on criteria determined by our Board of Directors, including, but not limited to, the satisfaction of minimum performance standards,
and the achievement of budgetary and other objectives, set by our Board of Directors in its sole and absolute discretion. None of our
named executive officers received a cash bonus during 2024.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Director Compensation</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">We did not pay any compensation to our directors
or issue any equity awards to our directors during 2024.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Executive Compensation</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Complete Solaria&#8217;s Compensation Committee
oversees the compensation policies, plans and programs and reviews and determines compensation to be paid to executive officers, directors
and other senior management, as appropriate. The compensation policies followed by Complete Solaria are intended to provide for compensation
that is sufficient to attract, motivate and retain executives of Complete Solaria and potential other individuals and to establish an
appropriate relationship between executive compensation and the creation of stockholder value.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Nonqualified Deferred Compensation</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Complete Solaria&#8217;s named executive officers
did not participate in, or earn any benefits under, any nonqualified deferred compensation plan sponsored by Complete Solaria during 2024.
The Board of Directors may elect to provide officers and other employees with nonqualified deferred compensation benefits in the future
if it determines that doing so is in the Company&#8217;s best interests.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Pension Benefits</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Complete Solaria&#8217;s named executive officers
did not participate in, or otherwise receive any benefits under, any pension or retirement plan sponsored by Complete Solaria during 2024.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>Employee Benefit Plans</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Equity-based compensation has been and will continue
to be an important foundation in executive compensation packages as Complete Solaria believes it is important to maintain a strong link
between executive incentives and the creation of stockholder value. Complete Solaria believes that performance and equity-based compensation
can be an important component of the total executive compensation package for maximizing stockholder value while, at the same time, attracting,
motivating and retaining high-quality executives. In July&#160;2023, our Board of Directors adopted the 2023 Equity Incentive Plan (the
&#8220;<b><i>2023 Plan</i></b>&#8221;) and the Employee Stock Purchase Plan (the &#8220;<b><i>ESPP</i>&#8221;</b>). The 2023&#160;Plan
and the ESPP became effective immediately upon the Closing of the Business Combination. Below is a description of the 2023 Plan, the ESPP,
Complete Solaria&#8217;s 2022 Stock Plan (the &#8220;<b><i>2022 Plan</i></b>&#8221;), Complete Solaria&#8217;s 2011 Stock Plan (the &#8220;<b><i>2011
Plan</i></b>&#8221;), Complete Solaria&#8217;s 2016 Stock Plan (the &#8220;<b><i>2016 Plan</i></b>&#8221;) and Complete Solaria&#8217;s
2006 Stock Plan (the &#8220;<b><i>2006 Plan</i></b>&#8221;). The 2022 Plan is the successor of the 2021 Stock Plan of Legacy Complete
Solaria, which was amended and assumed by Complete Solaria in connection with a merger transaction completed prior to the Business Combination
by Complete Solaria (the &#8220;<b><i>Prior Transaction</i></b>&#8221;). The 2011 Plan is the 2011 Stock Plan of Legacy Complete Solaria
that was assumed by Complete Solaria in the Prior Transaction. The 2022 Plan, 2016 Plan, 2011 Plan and 2006 Plan are collectively referred
to as the &#8220;<b><i>Legacy Plans</i></b>&#8221;.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">The 2016 Plan and the 2006 Plan are the stock
plans of Solaria that were assumed by Complete Solaria in the Prior Transaction.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"></p><div>

</div><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 122; Value: 52 --><div>
    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt"><!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->65<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
    </div><div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 12pt"><p style="margin: 0pt"></p></div><div>
    </div><!-- Field: /Page --><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>2023 Plan</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">In July 2023, our board of directors adopted and our stockholders approved
the 2023 Plan. The 2023 Plan became effective immediately upon the closing.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><i>Eligibility.</i>&#160;Any individual who is an employee of Complete
Solaria or any of its affiliates, or any person who provides services to Complete Solaria or its affiliates, including consultants and
members of Complete Solaria&#8217;s Board, is eligible to receive awards under the 2023 Plan at the discretion of the plan administrator.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><i>Awards</i>. The 2023 Plan provides for the grant of incentive
stock options (&#8220;<b><i>ISOs</i></b>&#8221;), within the meaning of Section 422 of the Code to employees, including employees of any
parent or subsidiary, and for the grant of nonstatutory stock options (&#8220;<b><i>NSOs</i></b>&#8221;), stock appreciation rights, restricted
stock awards, restricted stock unit awards, performance awards and other forms of awards to employees, directors and consultants, including
employees and consultants of Complete Solaria&#8217;s affiliates.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><i>Authorized Shares</i>. Current,
a maximum number of 11,147,034 (after giving effect to the evergreen increase effective as of January 1, 2025) of shares of Complete Solaria
Common Stock may be issued under the 2023 Plan. In addition, the number of shares of Complete Solaria Common Stock reserved for issuance
under the 2023 Plan will automatically increase on January 1 of each year, starting on January 1, 2024 and ending on January 1, 2033,
in an amount equal to the lesser of (1) 4% of the total number of shares of Complete Solaria&#8217;s Common Stock outstanding on December
31 of the preceding year, or (2) a lesser number of shares of Complete Solaria Common Stock determined by Complete Solaria&#8217;s Board
prior to the date of the increase. The maximum number of shares of Complete Solaria Common Stock that may be issued on the exercise of
ISOs under the 2023 Plan is three times the number of shares available for issuance upon the 2023 Plan becoming effective.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><b>&#160;</b></p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">The unused shares subject to stock awards granted under the 2023 Plan
that expire, lapse or are terminated, exchanged for or settled in cash, surrendered, repurchased, canceled without having been fully exercised
or forfeited, in any case, in a manner that results in Complete Solaria acquiring shares covered by the stock award at a price not greater
than the price (as adjusted pursuant to the 2023 Plan) paid by the participant for such shares or not issuing any shares covered by the
stock award, will, as applicable, become or again be available for stock award grants under the 2023 Plan.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><i>Non-Employee Director Compensation Limit</i>.&#8195; The aggregate
value of all compensation granted or paid to any non-employee director with respect to any calendar year, including awards granted and
cash fees paid to such non-employee director, will not exceed (1) $1,000,000 in total value or (2) if such non-employee director is first
appointed or elected to Complete Solaria&#8217;s Board during such calendar year, $1,500,000 in total value, in each case, calculating
the value of any equity awards based on the grant date fair value of such equity awards for financial reporting purposes.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><i>Plan Administration</i>. Complete Solaria&#8217;s Board, or a duly
authorized committee thereof, will administer the 2023 Plan and is referred to as the &#8220;plan administrator&#8221; herein. Complete
Solaria&#8217;s Board may also delegate to one or more of Complete Solaria&#8217;s officers the authority to (1) designate employees (other
than officers) to receive specified stock awards and (2) determine the number of shares subject to such stock awards. Under the 2023 Plan,
the Complete Solaria Board has the authority to determine award recipients, grant dates, the numbers and types of stock awards to be granted,
the applicable fair market value, and the provisions of each stock award, including the period of exercisability and the vesting schedule
applicable to a stock award.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><i>Stock Options</i>. ISOs and NSOs are granted under stock option
agreements adopted by the plan administrator. The plan administrator determines the exercise price for stock options, within the terms
and conditions of the 2023 Plan, provided that the exercise price of a stock option generally cannot be less than 100% of the fair market
value of a share of Complete Solaria Common Stock on the date of grant. Options granted under the 2023 Plan vest at the rate specified
in the stock option agreement as determined by the plan administrator.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0"></p><div>

</div><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 123; Value: 52 --><div>
    </div><div style="margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; border-bottom: Black 1.5pt solid"><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; text-align: center; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt"><!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->66<!-- Field: /Sequence --></p></div><div>
    </div><div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt; margin-bottom: 12pt"><p style="margin: 0pt"></p></div><div>
    </div><!-- Field: /Page --><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">The plan administrator determines the term of stock options granted
under the 2023 Plan, up to a maximum of 10 years. Unless the terms of an optionholder&#8217;s stock option agreement provide otherwise
or as otherwise provided by the plan administrator, if an optionholder&#8217;s service relationship with Complete Solaria or any of Complete
Solaria&#8217;s affiliates ceases for any reason other than disability, death, or cause, the optionholder may generally exercise any vested
options for a period of three months following the cessation of service. This period may be extended in the event that exercise of the
option is prohibited by applicable securities laws. Unless the terms of an optionholder&#8217;s stock option agreement provide otherwise
or as otherwise provided by the plan administrator, if an optionholder&#8217;s service relationship with Complete Solaria or any of Complete
Solaria&#8217;s affiliates ceases due to death or disability, or an optionholder dies within a certain period following cessation of service,
the optionholder or a beneficiary may generally exercise any vested options for a period of 18 months following the date of death, or
12 months following the date of disability. In the event of a termination for cause, options generally terminate upon the termination
date. In no event may an option be exercised beyond the expiration of its term.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Acceptable consideration for the purchase
of Complete Solaria Common Stock issued upon the exercise of a stock option will be determined by the plan administrator and may include
(1) cash, check, bank draft or money order, (2) a broker-assisted cashless exercise, (3) the tender of shares of Complete Solaria Common
Stock previously owned by the optionholder, (4) a net exercise of the option if it is an NSO or (5) other legal consideration approved
by the plan administrator.</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify">Unless the plan administrator provides otherwise, options and
stock appreciation rights generally are not transferable except by will or the laws of descent and distribution. Subject to approval of
the plan administrator or a duly authorized officer, an option may be transferred pursuant to a domestic relations order.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><i>Tax Limitations on ISOs</i>. The aggregate fair market
value, determined at the time of grant, of Complete Solaria&#8217;s Common Stock with respect to ISOs that are exercisable for the first
time by an award holder during any calendar year under all of Complete Solaria&#8217;s stock plans may not exceed $100,000. Options or
portions thereof that exceed such limit will generally be treated as NSOs. No ISO may be granted to any person who, at the time of the
grant, owns or is deemed to own stock possessing more than 10% of Complete Solaria&#8217;s total combined voting power or that of any
of Complete Solaria&#8217;s parent or subsidiary corporations unless (1) the option exercise price is at least 110% of the fair market
value of the stock subject to the option on the date of grant and (2) the term of the ISO does not exceed five years from the date of
grant.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><i>Restricted Stock Unit Awards</i>. Restricted stock unit awards
are granted under restricted stock unit award agreements adopted by the plan administrator. Restricted stock unit awards will generally
be granted in consideration for a participant&#8217;s services, but may be granted in consideration for any form of legal consideration
that may be acceptable to the plan administrator and permissible under applicable law. A restricted stock unit award may be settled by
cash, delivery of shares of Complete Solaria Common Stock, a combination of cash and shares of Complete Solaria Common Stock as determined
by the plan administrator, or in any other form of consideration set forth in the restricted stock unit award agreement. Additionally,
dividend equivalents may be credited in respect of shares covered by a restricted stock unit award. Except as otherwise provided in the
applicable award agreement or by the plan administrator, restricted stock unit awards that have not vested will be forfeited once the
participant&#8217;s continuous service ends for any reason.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><i>Restricted Stock Awards</i>. Restricted stock awards are granted
under restricted stock award agreements adopted by the plan administrator. A restricted stock award may be awarded in consideration for
cash, check, bank draft or money order, services to us, or any other form of legal consideration that may be acceptable to the plan administrator
and permissible under applicable law. The plan administrator determines the terms and conditions of restricted stock awards, including
vesting and forfeiture terms. If a participant&#8217;s service relationship with Complete Solaria ends for any reason, Complete Solaria
may receive any or all of the shares of Complete Solaria Common Stock held by the participant that have not vested as of the date the
participant terminates service with Complete Solaria through a forfeiture condition or a repurchase right.</p><div>

</div><p style="text-align: justify; font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0">&#160;</p><div>

</div><p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0; text-indent: 0.5in; text-align: justify"><i>Stock Appreciation Rights</i>. Stock appreciation rights are
granted under stock appreciation right agreements adopted by the plan administrator. The plan administrator determines the strike price
for a stock appreciation right, which generally cannot be less than 100% of the fair market value of Complete Solaria Common Stock on
